data.title: Trump to sign executive order directing Department of Education shutdown - report
data.description:
President Donald Trump is planning to sign an executive order on Thursday calling for the closure of the Department of Education, the Associated Press reported, citing an unnamed White House official.

The order will direct Education Secretary Linda McMahon to "take all necessary steps to facilitate the closure (of) the Department of Education and return education authority to the States, while continuing to ensure the effective and uninterrupted delivery of services, programs, and benefits on which Americans rely."

Eliminating the department would require 60 Senate votes to pass. With Republicans holding a 53-47 majority in the Senate, seven Democrats would need to support the move - which is seen as unlikely.

"At a time of massive income and wealth inequality, it is a national disgrace that the Trump administration is attempting to illegally abolish the Department of Education," 38 Democrat lawmakers wrote in a letter to McMahon. "The administration also says it wants to 'return education back to the states.' Let us be very clear - public education is already run by states and local school boards."

The letter followed the Department of Education's announcement last week that it would lay off nearly half of its workforce. Apart from supporting low-income school districts and students with disabilities, the department oversees the over $1.6T in student loans held by Americans.

Student loans-related stocks: Nelnet (NYSE:NNI), Global Payments (NYSE:GPN), SoFi Technologies (NASDAQ:SOFI), Navient (NASDAQ:NAVI), SLM Corp. (NASDAQ:SLM).

data.title: Affirm and Adyen Expand Partnership to the UK
Biggest stock movers Thursday: YY, FIVE, and more
data.description:
Stock futures rose Thursday morning after the Federal Reserve left interest rates unchanged and maintained its projection for two rate cuts in 2025.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Five Below (NASDAQ:FIVE) +12% – Shares surged after strong Q4 results, driven by store expansion. The company increased its store count by 14.7% Y/Y to 1,771 locations across 44 states, adding 22 net new stores. For Q1, Five Below (NASDAQ:FIVE) expects sales between $905M and $925M (midpoint: $915M), above the $898M consensus. Adjusted diluted EPS is projected to be between $0.50 and $0.61 (midpoint: $0.555), exceeding the consensus of $0.53. The company also plans to open 50 new stores in the quarter.
  • ProAssurance (NYSE:PRA) +49% – Shares soared after The Doctors Company announced a definitive agreement to acquire ProAssurance. Under the deal, ProAssurance shareholders will receive $25.00 per share in cash, a ~60% premium to the March 18 closing price. The ~$1.3B transaction will create a combined company with ~$12B in assets and is expected to close in 1H2026, with no financing conditions.

Biggest stock losers

  • Akebia Therapeutics (NASDAQ:AKBA) -24% – Shares tumbled after the company announced an underwritten public offering. All shares in the offering will be sold by Akebia, with an option for underwriters to purchase up to an additional 15% of the shares within 30 days. The offering is subject to market conditions, with no assurance of its completion, size, or terms.
  • JOYY (NASDAQ:YY) -10% – Shares plunged after mixed Q4 results and declining user metrics. Global average mobile MAUs dropped to 263.1M from 274.9M a year ago, with Bigo Live MAUs falling to 33.4M from 38.4M, Likee MAUs declining to 30.6M from 39.1M, and Hago MAUs decreasing to 3.6M from 4.6M. The total number of paying users for BIGO (including Bigo Live, Likee, and imo) declined to 1.54M from 1.67M, while ARPPU fell to $237.1 from $244.8. For Q1 2025, JOYY expects net revenue between $482M and $490M, below the $492.33M consensus.

data.title: Sanofi strikes autoimmune drug deal for up to $1.9B
data.description:
Sanofi (NASDAQ:SNY) has agreed to acquire an immunology drug from clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company Dren Bio for up to $1.9B.

The French pharmaceutical company will acquire DR-0201, a CD20-directed bispecific antibody currently being evaluated in a Phase 1 trial in B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma patients and is expanding into various autoimmune indications.

The drug candidate has shown a favorable preclinical safety profile in multiple non-human primate studies, with no cytokine release syndrome or neurotoxicity.

Under the terms of the merger agreement, Sanofi will acquire DR-0201 through the acquisition of the Dren Bio affiliate Dren-0201 for an upfront payment of $600M and potential future payments totaling $1.3B upon achievement of certain development and launch milestones.

The companies expect the transaction to close during the second quarter of 2025.

data.title: Enerflex CEO Marc Rossiter steps down; appoints Preet Dhindsa as interim CEO and reaffirms FY25 outlook
European markets mixed as traders await central bank verdicts
Alvotech acquires Xbrane’s R&D operations in Sweden and biosimilar candidate XB003
data.description:
Alvotech (NASDAQ:ALVO) Thursday announced the acquisition of Xbrane Biopharma AB’s R&D operations and a biosimilar candidate, establishing a footprint in the Swedish life science sector.

The acquisition’s purchase price equals a value of about SEK 275 million ($27 million) and will be payable as SEK 102.2 million in cash at closing and by assumption of SEK 172.8 million in debt and accounts payable.

The acquisition includes Xbrane’s R&D operations based in Campus Solna, at the Karolinska Institute outside Stockholm, Sweden, as well as the biosimilar candidate XB003, referencing Cimzia.

Xbrane retains other pre-clinical assets and will focus on the commercialization of this portfolio, Alvotech (NASDAQ:ALVO) said, adding that it also intends to explore the possibility of a listing of Swedish Depository Receipts, equity share equivalents, on Nasdaq Stockholm, in the future.

The creditors have agreed to accept payment for SEK 152.8 million of the debt with Alvotech equity shares.

data.title: Trump calls on Fed to cut rates as tariffs start to 'ease' into economy
data.description:
President Donald Trump said the Federal Reserve should cut interest rates as tariffs start to "ease" into the economy, after Fed Chair Jerome Powell said tariffs may delay further progress in bringing inflation to the 2% target.

"The Fed would be MUCH better off CUTTING RATES as U.S. tariffs start to transition (ease!) their way into the economy," Trump posted on his social media app Truth Social. "Do the right thing. April 2nd is Liberation Day in America!!!"

April 2 is when Trump will unveil new reciprocal tariffs on various countries based on the rates they charge for American goods. The scope of the new tariffs remains unclear.

The Fed on Wednesday kept its policy interest rate unchanged at 4.25%-4.50%, as expected. Fed officials still expect two 25-basis-point rate cuts this year, but forecast slower economic growth and higher inflation.

In his post-decision presser, Powell said the tariff effect should be "transitory," and long-term inflation expectations are fairly stable overall. But he acknowledged the high level of uncertainty in the economy. 

"Staying in wait-and-see mode is viewed as a reasonable compromise," said Kathy Jones, chief fixed income strategist at Schwab Center for Financial Research. "Given the cross currents facing the economy, we do not expect a change in interest rate policy from the Fed until later this year, if at all."

data.title: E3 Lithium finalizes $4.4 million funding agreement
data.description:
• E3 Lithium (OTCQX:EEMMF) Thursday said that it has executed the contribution agreement for $4.40 million in non-repayable funding from the Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund.
  • Under the agreement terms, E3 Lithium will receive reimbursement for 50% of eligible expenditures up to the total $4.4 million allocation.
  • The investment will be used to undertake preconstruction work on the necessary transportation and energy infrastructure to develop the Clearwater Project.
  • E3 previously announced on March 3, 2025, conditional approval for up to $4.7 million in funding from CMIF.
